title: Why Did Meta Change Its Mind? Zuckerberg Is Now Backing The Kids Online Safety Act After Years Of Opposition
author: Namrata Sen
description: Meta Platforms has reversed its previous opposition to the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA), now supporting the bill that mandates tech firms to establish online safety measures for children. This shift comes as Meta sees KOSA as a middle ground that would supersede fragmented state AI regulations and shift some child safety responsibility to app stores like Apple and Google. The move reflects growing global pressure to regulate online child safety, with the UK and Canada also introducing new restrictions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Meta's reversal on KOSA support is strategically motivated rather than principled—the company now backs the bill because it shifts responsibility to app stores and supersedes state regulations, which benefits Meta's business interests. While this shows adaptability to regulatory pressure, it's a calculated move rather than a positive development for child safety advocacy.

Apple faces increased regulatory burden through KOSA's age-verification mandate for app stores. The company has raised privacy concerns about age verification requirements, and the bill would shift child safety responsibility to Apple's platform, creating operational and compliance challenges.

Similar to Apple, Google faces regulatory pressure from KOSA's age-verification requirements for its app store and would bear increased responsibility for child safety measures on its platforms, creating compliance and operational burdens.

title: Stock Market Today, June 16: Snap Falls After Launch of $2,195 AR Glasses
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Snap's stock fell 9.63% on June 16, 2026, following the launch of its $2,195 SPECS augmented reality glasses. Investors questioned whether consumer demand would justify the heavy AR spending, especially given the product's high price point (roughly 3x Meta's Ray-Ban glasses) and Snap's lack of profitability. The broader market saw the S&P 500 decline 0.55% and Nasdaq drop 1.15%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 9.63% due to investor concerns about the $2,195 SPECS glasses being overpriced compared to competitors, heavy AR spending without profitability, and uncertainty about consumer demand. The company has declined 79% since its 2017 IPO.
Stock rose 1.13% as investors viewed Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses as a more competitively priced alternative to Snap's SPECS, positioning Meta favorably in the AR market competition.
Stock declined 0.68%, reflecting mixed sentiment across social media platforms on the day, though not directly related to the Snap news.

title: Qualcomm's CEO Reveals 40+ AI Devices For A Post-App Future — Stock Gains 4% As $10 Billion Tenstorrent Deal Rumours Swirl
author: Namrata Sen
description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon announced the company is developing over 40 AI-powered devices including smart glasses, jewelry, earbuds, and watches that will function as personal AI agents. The announcement drove Qualcomm's stock up 4% in pre-market trading. Reports indicate Qualcomm is in talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for $8-10 billion to support its AI device strategy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO announced ambitious 40+ AI device pipeline, stock gained 4% on the news, pursuing strategic acquisition of Tenstorrent to strengthen AI capabilities, and positioning itself as a leader in next-generation consumer AI hardware.
CEO suggested that rise of AI devices could bring new players into consumer market, posing challenges for major smartphone manufacturers like Apple.
Multi-year deal with Qualcomm to power future generations of Snap Specs with advanced Snapdragon SoC solutions positions the company in the emerging AI wearables market.

title: Meta, Google, Snap In Spotlight As UK Reportedly Eyes Crackdown On 'Harmful' Social Media For Under-16s
author: Namrata Sen
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is reportedly planning to announce a ban on 'harmful' social media platforms for children under 16, following similar moves by Australia and growing global scrutiny. The decision comes after consultations with grieving parents and aims to address concerns about mental health, online safety, and sextortion. Switzerland has also signaled support for stricter safeguards, with 94% of survey respondents backing restrictions on minors' social media access.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Meta's Instagram and Facebook platforms are directly targeted by proposed UK restrictions on harmful social media for minors, which could significantly impact user growth and engagement in a key demographic.

Google's YouTube platform is flagged in Australia's regulatory enforcement and would be affected by UK restrictions on social media access for under-16s, potentially limiting advertising revenue from younger users.

Google's YouTube platform is flagged in Australia's regulatory enforcement and would be affected by UK restrictions on social media access for under-16s, potentially limiting advertising revenue from younger users.

Snapchat is explicitly mentioned as facing regulatory scrutiny in Australia and would be impacted by proposed UK bans on harmful social media platforms for minors, threatening its user base and revenue.

title: NCPC Urges Rejection of Weak House Bill on Kids Online Safety
author: National Crime Prevention Council
description: The National Crime Prevention Council opposes the House's Kids Internet and Digital Safety Act (H.R. 7757), arguing it strips states of child protection powers and lacks 'duty to care' provisions found in the Senate version. The NCPC criticizes the bill as a 'fig leaf' benefiting social media companies, noting over 40 states are suing Meta, Snapchat, Google, and TikTok for harms including fake drug sales and mental health damage.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Meta is named as a defendant in lawsuits by 40+ states for designing products that function as drug markets and cause mental health harms. The article criticizes legislation that would benefit the company.
Snapchat is listed alongside Meta and other platforms in lawsuits for similar harms including facilitating criminal activity and mental health damage to users.
Google is named as a defendant in multi-state lawsuits for platform design that allegedly facilitates drug sales, sex trafficking, and causes mental health harms to children.
Google is named as a defendant in multi-state lawsuits for platform design that allegedly facilitates drug sales, sex trafficking, and causes mental health harms to children.
